info.title: Sungrow iSolarCloud OpenAPI
info.description: Unofficial OpenAPI description of the Sungrow iSolarCloud OpenAPI, generated from the official developer-portal documentation (https://developer-api.isolarcloud.com). All requests are POST with a JSON body that also carries `appkey` and `lang`; authentication uses `x-access-key` + a Bearer token. Not affiliated with or endorsed by Sungrow.

post.description: Query current user's charger information

currentType.description: Charger Type: AC = Alternating Current (AC), DC = Direct Current (DC)

chargerPlugRunStatus.description: Connector Status: FAULT-Fault, CHARGING-Charging, PARK-Completed, BOOKED-Booked, MAINTAIN-Under Maintenance, BMS_STARTING-Preparing, FREE-Idle, SUSPENDED_EV-Suspended by EV, SUSPENDED_EVSE-Suspended by EVSE

appkey.description: appKey authorized by the server to the calling party

noce.description: 32-character random alphanumeric string, unique per request

post.description: Query charger list under current user's station

currentType.description: Charger Type: AC = Alternating Current (AC), DC = Direct Current (DC)

appkey.description: appKey authorized by the server to the calling party

noce.description: 32-character random alphanumeric string, unique per request

post.description: Refresh Token Interface

code.description: 1: Succeeded 2: No authorization information -1: Authorization server token refresh failed  -2: Refresh_token expired 4: Invalid request 5: Invalid refresh token

post.description: Obtain token via authorization code

result_code.description:
Status Code
1: Success
2: Invalid authorization code
3: Unauthorized access
4: Invalid client
5: The redirect address does not match
6: The grant type is not supported

auth_ps_list.description: List of authorized plants. The returned information is plant ID.

code.description: Authorization Code

token_scope_mode.description:
Default value: global； 
global：Gets the global access token for the current application；authorization_bound：Returns the Access Token matched to the authorization relationship of the current authorization code

post.summary: Disarm the power plant channel partner
post.description: Remove channel partners from power plants and support batch operations.

result_code.description: Operation result code: 1: discontinuing successfully 0: discontinuing failed

share_type.description: Permission types of added channel partners: 1: browse permission 2: manage permission

is_channel_partner.description: Add as channel partner, fixed transfer 1

user_email.description: The email address and mobile phone number of the person to be cancelled need to be passed

user_mobile_tel.description: Mobile phone number Email and mobile phone number need to be passed

post.summary: Retrieve the corresponding Mqtt information based on the appkey
post.description: This interface obtains the Mqtt public key, Mqtt address, username, password, and other information corresponding to the appkey in the input parameters.

mqtt_type.description: The MQTT protocol types supported by this appkey are: 1: TCP, 2: WS, 3: WS and TCP

mqtt_url_list.description: Mqtt connection address (current set of connection addresses supported by Appey)

code.description: Operation code: 1: Successfully obtained; 0: Acquisition failed; 2: This appkey does not support Mqtt message subscription;

post.description: Get device history data, currently, we only provide data in most recent one hour. Only devices with live data upload function enabled support history data query.

code.description: Error Code (0: The device is not an inverter or does not exist; 1: The device does not belong to the user; 2: The device does not support live data; 3: Device offline)

msgTime.description: Real time data (with time zone)(format: yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ssXXX)

recvTime.description: Data reception time (with time zone)(format: yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ssXXX)

post.description: Sends instructions to the inverter to enable live data upload. Supports subscription to data via MQTT.

code.description: Error Code (0: The device is not an inverter or does not exist; 1: The device does not belong to the user; 2: The device does not support live data; 3: Device offline)

second.description: Upload Frequency in Seconds (60s by default, cannot be less than 5s). Example: 10 means upload once every 10 seconds

post.description: Sends instructions to the inverter to disable live data upload

code.description: Error Code (0: The device is not an inverter or does not exist; 1: The device does not belong to the user; 2: The device does not support live data; 3: Device offline)

post.summary: Batch querying basic information about power plants
post.description: Batch querying basic information about power plants

code.description: Interface response code, 0- query failed, the interface is abnormal, 1- success, 3- there is user_id in the input parameter, and the user_id is inconsistent with the token user, 4- ps_ids in the input parameter is empty, the power plant is not queried through sns, 5- The number of power plants or sns exceeds the maximum limit, 6- Basic information about the power plant is not found according to ps_ids. 7- All the power plants entered into ps_ids are not the power plants of the current user

is_not_belong_to_user.description: Enter the set of power plant ids that do not belong to the current user in ps_ids

ps_price_kwh.description: The current electricity price per kWh of the power plant

ps_fault_status.description: power plant fault status 1: fault, 2: alarm, 4: normal

build_status.description: Plant Construction Status (0: Not Started; 1: Under Construction; 2: Connected; 3: Planned; 4: Not Connected)

ps_type.description: Plant Type: 1: Utility Plant; 3: Distributed PV Plant; 4: Residential PV Plant; 5: Residential Energy Storage Plant; 6: Village Plant; 7: Distributed Energy Storage Plant; 8: Poverty Alleviation Plant; 9: Wind Power Plant; 12: C&I Energy Storage Plant

ps_price.description: The current electricity price per Wh of the power plant

share_user_type.description: Type of sharing of power plant: 1: owner sharing 2: installer sharing Other: non-sharing power plant

share_type.description: Sharing type of the power plant 1: sharing type (browsing rights) 2: sharing type (management rights) 0: non-sharing power plant (my power plant)

connect_type.description: Grid Connection Type: (1: Full Grid; 2: Self-Generated and Self-Used (Surplus Power); 3: Self-Generated and Self-Used (No Feed Network); 4: Off-Grid)

ps_ids.description: The plant ID, separate multiple values with commas (can query up to 20 IDs—either the ps_ids or sns must be provided; when both are passed, the ps_ids is queried; the sns is only used if the ps_ids query returns empty)

sns.description: Communication Device SN (separate multiple values with commas (you can query up to 20 SNs); the ps_ids is queried and the sns is only used if the ps_ids query returns empty)

is_get_ps_remarks.description: Get Plant Remarks?: (1: Yes; defaults to no if not passed)

post.summary: Query information about the communication device based on the device SN
post.description: Query information about the communication device based on the device SN

share_type.description: Share type of the power plant corresponding to the device sn: 1: query the power plant that has the browsing permission 2: query the power plant that has the management permission 0: query the own power plant If multiple types are separated by commas (,), the own power plant is queried by default

post.description: Example Query the specified attribute measurement points of N devices of the same type in a power plant.

code.description: Result code: 1: The query succeeds 2: the incoming parameter is empty 3: the power plant does not exist under the user 4: No data is queried

unit.description: The unit corresponding to the property value

point_id_list.description: Measuring Point ID Set (see Common Measuring Points for specific open measuring point definitions)

ps_key_list.description: Device ps_key Collection (devices of the same type)

post.summary: Query Device List of the Plant
post.description: Query device list by plant ID.

dev_fault_status.description: Device Fault Status: (1: Fault; 2: Alarm; 4: Normal)

ps_key.description: Device ps_key (used to query device data)

pvd_version.description: String Monitoring Unit Software Version No.

system_version.description: Communication Device Hardware Platform Version No.

is_virtual_unit.description: Querying Virtual Device? (1: Virtual Device; 0: Physical Device; defaults to 0). Plants, grid connection points, and units are considered virtual devices

device_type_list.description: Equipment Type List (such as: 11: Plant; 1: Inverter; 3: Grid Connection Point; 17: Unit; defaults to all when empty). Refer to Appendix: Device Type Definitions for details

is_get_firmware_version.description: Get Device Firmware Info? (0: No; 1: Yes; defaults to 0)

post.description: Example Query the device list of a user

dev_fault_status.description: Device Fault Status: (1: Fault; 2: Alarm; 4: Normal)

ps_key.description: Device ps_key (used to query device data)

is_virtual_unit.description: Querying Virtual Device? (1: Virtual Device; 0: Physical Device; defaults to 0). Plants, grid connection points, and units are considered virtual devices

device_type_list.description: Equipment Type List (such as: 11: Plant; 1: Inverter; 3: Grid Connection Point; 17: Unit; defaults to all when empty). Refer to Appendix: Device Type Definitions for details

is_get_firmware_version.description: Get Device Firmware Info? (0: No; 1: Yes; defaults to 0)

share_type.description: Share type of the power plant: 1: query the power plant that has the browse permission 2: query the power plant that has the management permission 0: query the power plant that has the own power plant. Use commas (,) to separate multiple types. All types are queried by default

post.summary: Query Historical Minute-Level Measuring Point Data of Devices of the Same Type
post.description: Query the minute-level data of device measuring points based on the ps_key and measuring point ID of devices of the same type and the specified time interval (time zone where the plant is located). Supports time intervals of up to 3 hours and up to 50 devices. The endpoint will not return measuring points with no data. Supports up to 50 measuring points per request. Historical data endpoints do not return data for the current day.

{p+pointid}.description: Measuring Point Value (output parameters are as follows: p1, p83022)

time_stamp.description: Time (time zone where the plant is located)

end_time_stamp.description: End Time (time zone where the plant is located; format: yyyyMMddHHmmss)

minute_interval.description: Duration Interval in Minutes (15 means measuring point data is queried every 15 minutes; if the parameter is not passed or empty, measuring point data is queried every 5 minutes)

points.description: p+ Measuring Point ID (such as p3022,p3024, separate multiple measuring points with commas; see Common Measuring Points for specific open measuring point definitions)

ps_key_list.description: Device ps_key Collection (devices of the same type)

start_time_stamp.description: Start Time (time zone where the plant is located; format: yyyyMMddHHmmss)

is_get_data_acquisition_time.description: Return Measuring Point Dictionary? (0: No; 1: Yes; defaults to no)

post.summary: Query Historical Daily/Monthly/Yearly Measuring Point Data of Devices of the Same Type
post.description:
Query the daily/monthly/yearly data of device measuring points based on the start time, end time, and ps_key of devices of the same type. Supports queries of multiple measuring points and up to 50 devices.

Note: This endpoint always returns the smallest unit of data. For example, Wh for yield and W for power. Supports up to 100 days for daily data queries, 24 months for monthly data queries, and 5 years for yearly data queries. Historical data endpoints do not return data for the current day.

{data_type}.description: The query data_type (such as: 1). Refers to the data_type input parameter

data_point.description: The format is p+measuring point, separating multiple measuring points with commas. Example: If the ps_key of the queried device is an inverter, then p1 represents the inverter yield (in Wh), p2 represents the inverter cumulative yield (in Wh), and p24 represents the inverter total active power (in W). See  Common Measuring Points for specific open measuring point definitions.

data_type.description: 1: Mean; 2: Peak; 3: Trough; 4: Total (only monthly and yearly data has a "total" value). Separate multiple values with commas. If data can be aggregated, then: data_type is 2 when querying daily data, and 4 when querying monthly or yearly data

end_time.description: Note for End Time: When query_type is "day," the date format is yyyyMMdd. When query_type is "month," the date format is yyyyMM. When query_type is "year," the date format is yyyy.

ps_key_list.description: Device ps_key Collection (devices of the same type)

query_type.description: Query type: Daily data: 1 Monthly data: 2 Annual data: 3

start_time.description: Note for Start Time: When query_type is "day," the date format is yyyyMMdd. When query_type is "month," the date format is yyyyMM. When query_type is "year," the date format is yyyy.

post.summary: Query Device Real-Time Measuring Point Data
post.description:
Query real-time measuring point data by device ps_key or SN.

Note:
1. This endpoint always returns the smallest unit of data. For example, Wh for yield, W for power, A for current, and V for voltage.

2. The data refreshes about every 5 minutes. It is recommended that the endpoint call frequency is no less than 5 minutes.

dev_fault_status.description: Device fault alarm status 1: Fault 2: Alarm 4: normal

p+pointid.description: Measuring Point Value (p1 represents the value of point_id=1)

point_id_list.description: Measuring Point ID Set (see Common Measuring Points for specific open measuring point definitions)

ps_key_list.description: ps_key Collection (must pass the same device type)

sn_list.description: SN Collection (pass either the ps_key_list or sn_list)

post.summary: Query the device parameter setting history
post.description: Query the parameter setting history by the device UUID and parameter code.

returnValue.description: Returned value (indicates parameter readback values during parameter readback)

commandStatus.description: Task Status (1: Pending Execution; 2: Executing; 4: Success; 5: Failure; 6: Timeout; 9: Canceled)

setPrecision.description: Parameter precision (please refer to measuring point description for details)

unit.description: Parameter unit (please refer to measuring point description for details)

taskSource.description: Task source : 1 = Page delivery , 2 = Open API

createTime.description: Task detail creation time , format : yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss

updateTime.description: Task detail update time , format : yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss

paramCode.description: Parameter Code (refer to the control parameter code definition)

paramList.description: Parameter Code collection (refer to the control parameter code definition)  , Maximum Quantity: 100

startTime.description: Start Time , format : yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss . Start and end time span must not exceed 30 days

endTime.description: End Time , format : yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss  . Start and end time span must not exceed 30 days

post.summary: Example Query the configuration information of a device group
post.description: Example Query the configuration information of a device group

pv_value.description: Whether the current group string is configured 1: configured, 0: not configured

code.description: 1: success, 0: failure

ps_key_list.description: Device ps_key Collection (devices of the same type)

post.description: Query device fault alarm information.

fault_level.description: Fault level: 1: major 2: minor 3: minor 4: minor (multiple transmission separated by commas)

fault_type.description: Fault Type: (1: Fault; 2: Alarm; 3: Notice; 4: Suggestion; separate multiple values with commas)

fault_reason.description: The device's actual fault code (separate multiple values with "|")

process_status.description: Fault Handling Status: (1: Unconfirmed; 2: Pending; 3: Processing; 4: Resolved; 9: Closed)

share_type.description: Share type of the power plant: 1: query the power plant that has the browse permission 2: query the power plant that has the management permission 0: query the power plant that has the own power plant. Use commas (,) to separate multiple types. All types are queried by default

fault_code.description: Unique Fault Identifier (pass to query the fault alarm information)

fault_name.description: Fault Name (fuzzy search if not empty)

startTime.description: Start Time (format: yyyyMMddHHmm) can be empty. Note: When querying the faults with process_status=9 or 999, the time range must be limited to a single month and cannot span across months. If no time is provided, the query will default to faults for the current month.

endTime.description: End Time (format: yyyyMMddHHmm) can be empty. Note: When querying the faults with process_status=9 or 999, the time range must be limited to a single month and cannot span across months. If no time is provided, the query will default to faults for the current month.

process_status.description: Fault Status: (8: Unclosed; 9: Closed; 999: Unclosed and Closed (cannot query across months); queries for unclosed faults if empty)

fault_type.description: Fault Type: (1: Fault; 2: Alarm; 3: Notice; 4: Suggestion; separate multiple values with commas)

fault_level.description: Fault level: 1: major 2: minor 3: minor 4: minor (multiple transmission separated by commas)

ps_id.description: Plant ID (if the ps_id is not passed, the fault information of all authorized plants is queried by default)

post.summary: Query the historical datapoint datapoint of the optimizer
post.description: Query the historical measurement point datas of the optimizer according to the start time and end time, device ps_key, and data type to obtain the device datas related data, support multi-measurement point query. Note: The unit of the data returned by the interface is the smallest unit, such as the unit corresponding to the power generation data is the smallest unit wh, and the unit corresponding to the power data is the smallest unit w.

{data_type}.description: The query data_type (such as: 1). Refers to the data_type input parameter

ps_key_list.description: Optimizer ps_key, supports up to 100 at a time

data_point.description: The format is p+measuring point, separating multiple measuring points with commas. Example: If the ps_key of the queried device is an inverter, then p1 represents the inverter yield (in Wh), p2 represents the inverter cumulative yield (in Wh), and p24 represents the inverter total active power (in W). See  Common Measuring Points for specific open measuring point definitions.

start_time.description: Note for Start Time: When query_type is "day," the date format is yyyyMMdd. When query_type is "month," the date format is yyyyMM. When query_type is "year," the date format is yyyy.

end_time.description: Note for End Time: When query_type is "day," the date format is yyyyMMdd. When query_type is "month," the date format is yyyyMM. When query_type is "year," the date format is yyyy.

data_type.description: 1: Mean; 2: Peak; 3: Trough; 4: Total (only monthly and yearly data has a "total" value). Separate multiple values with commas. If data can be aggregated, then: data_type is 2 when querying daily data, and 4 when querying monthly or yearly data

query_type.description: Query type: Daily data: 1 Monthly data: 2 Annual data: 3

post.summary: Gets a list of optimizer devices under the power plant
post.description: Gets a list of optimizer devices under the power plant

code.description: 1: normal; 9: No permission

post.summary: Queries the minute level data of the historical stations of the optimizer
post.description: The query optimizer retrips the device minute data for use by third-party platforms. Note: The time span cannot exceed 30 days. Note: The unit of the data returned by the interface is the smallest unit, such as the unit corresponding to the power generation data is the smallest unit wh, and the unit corresponding to the power data is the smallest unit w.

{p+point_id}.description: Measuring Point Value (output parameters are as follows: p1, p83022)

time_stamp.description: Time (time zone where the plant is located)

points.description: Test point id. Multiple test points are separated by commas (,). Currently only supports p58101, p58103 p58104, p58105, p58106, p58107

ps_key_list.description: Optimizer ps_key, supports up to 100 at a time

minute_interval.description: Duration Interval in Minutes (15 means measuring point data is queried every 15 minutes; if the parameter is not passed or empty, measuring point data is queried every 5 minutes)

post.description: Query optimizer's real-time data API

p+point_id.description: Measuring Point Value (p1 represents the value of point_id=1)

ps_key_list.description: List of PS_keys of the optimizer, not more than 100 in a single query

point_id_list.description: Need to query the measuring point list, now support point list: 58101581 03581 04581 05581 06581

post.description: Query API calls

curr_hour_accessed_times.description: Number of access times in the current hour

per_day_access_times_config.description: The total number of times that can be accessed today

max_call_times.description: The maximum number of times that can be called

times.description: The number of calls remaining in that hour

today_accessed_times.description: Number of visits already made today

post.summary: Query the definition of the open measuring point of the sunshine cloud
post.description: You can query the information about the open telemetry, remote communication, and attribute measurement points of this type of device by device type. You can also query the information about the open telemetry points of this type by device type and device model ID.

storage_unit.description: Point data storage unit (telemetry and attribute points only)

type.description: Test point type: 1: remote signal test point 2: remote test point 5: attribute test point

device_model_id.description: Device Model ID When querying telemetry point information, if this parameter is specified, information about the telemetry points supported by the model is queried. If this parameter is not specified, all open measurement points of the device type are queried

post.summary: Query real-time measurement point data of a PV inverter
post.description: Query real-time measurement point data according to ps_key or SN of the PV inverter (the unit of data is the smallest basic unit). Note: The unit of the data returned by the interface is the smallest unit, such as the unit of power generation data is wh, the unit of power data is w, the unit of current is A, and the unit of voltage is V. This interface applies only to photovoltaic inverters. You can obtain the latest time of other types of devices by referring to the interface in 2.7 Querying the Historical Measurement Point Minute Data of the same type of device.

dev_fault_status.description: Device Fault Status: (1: Fault; 2: Alarm; 4: Normal)

fail_sn_list.description: Description of an invalid SN collection: This parameter is available only when the SN collection is queried

ps_key_list.description: Device ps_key Collection (devices of the same type)

sn_list.description: SN Collection (pass either the ps_key_list or sn_list)

post.description: Query the parameter configuration result via the parameter configuration task ID.

create_time_zone.description: Creation time in the user’s time zone. Format: yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ssXXX

over_time_zone.description: Task Completion Time in User Time Zone (format: yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ssXXX)

command_status.description: Parameter Instruction Status: (1: Pending Execution; 2: Executing; 4: Success; 5: Failure; 6: Timeout)

create_time_zone.description: Creation time in the user’s time zone. Format: yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ssXXX

param_code.description: Parameter Code (refer to the control parameter code definition)

return_value.description: Returned value (indicates parameter readback values during parameter readback)

set_precision.description: Parameter precision (please refer to measuring point description for details)

set_val_name.description: Name of values in the parameter range

unit.description: Parameter unit (please refer to measuring point description for details)

update_time_zone.description: Update Time in User Time Zone (format: yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ssXXX)

ps_fault_status.description: Plant Fault Status (1: Fault; 2: Alarm; 3: Normal)

ps_price.description: The current electricity price per Wh of the power plant

ps_price_kwh.description: The current electricity price per kWh of the power plant

ps_type.description: Plant Type: 1: Utility Plant; 3: Distributed PV Plant; 4: Residential PV Plant; 5: Residential Energy Storage Plant; 6: Village Plant; 7: Distributed Energy Storage Plant; 8: Poverty Alleviation Plant; 9: Wind Power Plant; 12: C&I Energy Storage Plant

share_type.description: Sharing type of the power plant 1: sharing type (browsing rights) 2: sharing type (management rights) 0: non-sharing power plant (my power plant)

share_user_type.description: Type of sharing of power plant: 1: owner sharing 2: installer sharing Other: non-sharing power plant

is_get_ps_remarks.description: Get Plant Remarks?: (1: Yes; defaults to no if not passed)

post.description: Query a user's plant list information or query the plant list information based on the user's organization ID. If you cannot find the required information, get the ps_id of the plant through this endpoint and call the "real-time plant data endpoint" while passing in the required data measurement point number.

build_status.description: Plant Construction Status (0: Not Started; 1: Under Construction; 2: Connected; 3: Planned; 4: Not Connected)

co2_reduce_update_time.description: The format of the last update time for co2 reduction today is yyyy-MM-dd'T 'hh :mm:ssXXX

connect_type.description: Grid Connection Type: (1: Full Grid; 2: Self-Generated and Self-Used (Surplus Power); 3: Self-Generated and Self-Used (No Feed Network); 4: Off-Grid)

co2_reduce_total_update_time.description: Last updated time for cumulative co2 emission reduction Format: yyyy-MM-dd'T 'hh :mm:ssXXX

curr_power_update_time.description: Last update time of the current power format: yyyy-MM-dd'T 'hh :mm:ssXXX

ps_fault_status.description: Plant Fault Status (1: Fault; 2: Alarm; 3: Normal)

ps_type.description: Plant Type (separate multiple values with commas, all values are queried by default): 1: Utility Plant; 3: Distributed PV Plant; 4: Residential PV Plant; 5: Residential Energy Storage Plant; 6: Village Plant; 7: Distributed Energy Storage Plant; 8: Poverty Alleviation Plant; 9: Wind Power Plant; 10: Utility Energy Storage Plant;12: C&I Energy Storage Plant

share_type.description: Sharing type of the power plant 1: sharing type (browsing rights) 2: sharing type (management rights) 0: non-sharing power plant (my power plant)

today_energy_update_time.description: Current energy yield Last updated time Format: yyyy-MM-dd'T 'hh :mm:ssXXX

total_capcity_update_time.description: Total installed capacity Last updated time Format: yyyy-MM-dd'T 'hh :mm:ssXXX

total_energy_update_time.description: Last updated time for accumulated energy output of the power plant format: yyyy-MM-dd'T 'hh :mm:ssXXX

ps_type.description: Plant Type (separate multiple values with commas, all values are queried by default): 1: Utility Plant; 3: Distributed PV Plant; 4: Residential PV Plant; 5: Residential Energy Storage Plant; 6: Village Plant; 7: Distributed Energy Storage Plant; 8: Poverty Alleviation Plant; 9: Wind Power Plant; 10: Utility Energy Storage Plant; 12: C&I Energy Storage Plant

share_type.description: Share type of the power plant: 1: query the power plant that has the browse permission 2: query the power plant that has the management permission 0: query the power plant that has the own power plant. Use commas (,) to separate multiple types. All types are queried by default

valid_flag.description: The power plant status to be queried is as follows: (1: normal, 2: disabled, 3: connecting) If the node is not reported, the power plant in status 1 is queried. If multiple statuses need to be queried, separate them using commas (,).

org_id.description: If querying by organization ID, then pass in the user_master_org_id output parameter returned by the corresponding login endpoint.

user_id.description: User id (The user must be a junior to the login account)

is_self_built.description: Self-built power plant 0- All 1- Self-built Default 0 All

post.description: Query Read-Only Parameter Definition
Read the task ID through the read-only parameter to query the task execution result.

command_status.description: Task Status (1: Pending Execution; 2: Executing; 4: Success; 5: Failure; 6: Timeout; 9: Canceled)

uuid.description: The device uuid (separate multiple values with commas). When set_type=2, only single-device operations are supported.

post.description: Login

login_state.description: Login status: -1: The account does not exist 0: the password is incorrect 1: the login is successful 2: The account is locked due to incorrect password 5: The account is locked by the administrator

post.description: The parameter Settings of the corresponding device are delivered through the interface.

check_result.description: Error Code (overall verification): (0: Verification failed and configuration could not be performed; 1: Task dispatch successful; 2: Duplicate parameter configuration measuring point; 3: Parameter limit exceeded; 4: Parameter configuration measuring point ID or value empty; 5: Device does not exist; 6: Parameter configuration template not set; 7: Device offline; 8: Timeout period exceeds range of [0-1800] seconds; 9: Operation repeated; 11: Only single-device operations are supported for parameter readback; 12: There is no support for negative values on this device's current version. Please upgrade to Logger1000 or reconfigure the settings.)

code.description: Error Code (single-device): (0: Verification failed and configuration could not be performed; 1: Task dispatch successful; 2: Duplicate parameter configuration measuring point; 3: Parameter limit exceeded; 4: Parameter configuration measuring point ID or value empty; 5: Device does not exist; 6: Parameter configuration template not set; 6-1: The device model doesn't support current param-setting code or user has no authority to this param-setting code; 7: Device offline)

uuid.description: The device uuid (separate multiple values with commas). When set_type=2, only single-device operations are supported.

not_support_negative_num_uuids.description: When check_result is 12, it returns the UUID of the device that does not support copy settings.

expire_second.description: Timeout Period of Parameter Configuration Task (in seconds, 0 to 1800)

param_list.description: Configured Parameter: The data type of each element is Map, the keys are param_code and set_value, and the values are the parameter's measuring point ID and configured value

set_type.description: Parameter Configuration Type: (0: Configuration; 2: Readback; defaults to 0)

uuid.description: The device uuid (separate multiple values with commas). When set_type=2, only single-device operations are supported.

post.description: Verifies whether the current device supports parameter configuration.

check_result.description: Verification Status (refers to overall parameter verification; 0: Failed; 1: Passed; 5: Device Does Not Exist; 6: Device Offline)
Verification Status (refers to single-device parameter verification; 0: Failed; 1: Passed; 5: Device Does Not Exist; 6: Device Offline)

uuid.description: The device uuid (separate multiple values with commas). When set_type=2, only single-device operations are supported.

post.summary: Retrieve the corresponding mqtt information based on the appkey
post.description: This interface obtains the mqtt public key, mqtt address, username, password, and other information corresponding to the appkey in the input parameters.

mqtt_type.description: The MQTT protocol types supported by this appkey are: 1: TCP, 2: WS, 3: WS and TCP

mqtt_url_list.description: Mqtt connection address (current set of connection addresses supported by Appey)

code.description: Operation code: 1: Successfully obtained; 0: Acquisition failed; 2: This appkey does not support Mqtt message subscription;

post.description: Get device history data, currently, we only provide data in most recent one hour. Only devices with live data upload function enabled support history data query.

code.description: Error Code (0: The device is not an inverter or does not exist; 1: The device does not belong to the user; 2: The device does not support live data; 3: Device offline)

msgTime.description: Real time data (with time zone)(format: yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ssXXX)

recvTime.description: Data reception time (with time zone)(format: yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ssXXX)

post.description: Sends instructions to the inverter to enable live data upload. Supports subscription to data via mqtt.

code.description: Error Code (0: The device is not an inverter or does not exist; 1: The device does not belong to the user; 2: The device does not support live data; 3: Device offline)

second.description: Upload Frequency in Seconds (60s by default).  No less than 60 seconds

post.description: Sends instructions to the inverter to disable live data upload

code.description: Error Code (0: Device Not Inverter or Does Not Exist; 1: Device Does Not Belong to User; 2: Device Does Not Support Live Data; 3: Device Offline)

post.summary: Query Device List of the Plant
post.description: Query device list by plant ID.

ps_key.description: Device ps_key (used to query device data)

dev_fault_status.description: Device Fault Status: (1: Fault; 2: Alarm; 4: Normal)

pvd_version.description: String Monitoring Unit Software Version No.

system_version.description: Communication Device Hardware Platform Version No.

is_virtual_unit.description: Querying Virtual Device? (1: Virtual Device; 0: Physical Device; defaults to 0). Plants, grid connection points, and units are considered virtual devices

device_type_list.description: Equipment Type List (such as: 11: Plant; 1: Inverter; 3: Grid Connection Point; 17: Unit; defaults to all when empty). Refer to Appendix: Device Type Definitions for details

is_get_firmware_version.description: Get Device Firmware Info? (0: No; 1: Yes; defaults to 0)

post.summary: Query Historical Daily/Monthly/Yearly Measuring Point Data of Devices of the Same Type
post.description:
Query the daily/monthly/yearly data of device measuring points based on the start time, end time, and ps_key of devices of the same type. Supports queries of multiple measuring points and up to 50 devices.

Note: This endpoint always returns the smallest unit of data. For example, Wh for yield and W for power. Supports up to 100 days for daily data queries, 24 months for monthly data queries, and 5 years for yearly data queries. Historical data endpoints do not return data for the current day.

{data_type}.description: The query data_type (such as: 1). Refers to the data_type input parameter

query_type.description: Query Type: (Query Daily Data: day; Query Monthly Data: month; Query Yearly Data: year)

data_type.description: 1: Mean; 2: Peak; 3: Trough; 4: Total (only monthly and yearly data has a "total" value). Separate multiple values with commas. If data can be aggregated, then: data_type is 2 when querying daily data, and 4 when querying monthly or yearly data

ps_key_list.description: Device ps_key Collection (devices of the same type)

data_point.description: The format is p+measuring point, separating multiple measuring points with commas. Example: If the ps_key of the queried device is an inverter, then p1 represents the inverter yield (in Wh), p2 represents the inverter cumulative yield (in Wh), and p24 represents the inverter total active power (in W). See Appendix 10 for specific open measuring point definitions.

start_time.description: Note for Start Time: When query_type is "day," the date format is yyyyMMdd. When query_type is "month," the date format is yyyyMM. When query_type is "year," the date format is yyyy.

end_time.description: Note for End Time: When query_type is "day," the date format is yyyyMMdd. When query_type is "month," the date format is yyyyMM. When query_type is "year," the date format is yyyy.

is_get_point_dict.description: Return Measuring Point Dictionary? (0: No; 1: Yes; defaults to no)

post.summary: Query Historical Minute-Level Measuring Point Data of Devices of the Same Type
post.description: Query the minute-level data of device measuring points based on the ps_key and measuring point ID of devices of the same type and the specified time interval (time zone where the plant is located). Supports time intervals of up to 3 hours and up to 50 devices. The endpoint will not return measuring points with no data. Supports up to 50 measuring points per request. Historical data endpoints do not return data for the current day.

{p+point_id}.description: Measuring Point Value (output parameters are as follows: p1, p83022)

time_stamp.description: Time (time zone where the plant is located)

start_time_stamp.description: Start Time (time zone where the plant is located; format: yyyyMMddHHmmss)

end_time_stamp.description: End Time (time zone where the plant is located; format: yyyyMMddHHmmss)

minute_interval.description: Duration Interval in Minutes (15 means measuring point data is queried every 15 minutes; if the parameter is not passed or empty, measuring point data is queried every 5 minutes)

ps_key_list.description: Device ps_key Collection (devices of the same type)

points.description: p+ Measuring Point ID (such as p3022 or p3024, separate multiple measuring points with commas; see Appendix 10 for specific open measuring point definitions)

is_get_point_dict.description: Return Measuring Point Dictionary? (0: No; 1: Yes; defaults to no)

post.summary: Query Device Real-Time Measuring Point Data
post.description:
Query real-time measuring point data by device ps_key or SN.

Note:
1. This endpoint always returns the smallest unit of data. For example, Wh for yield, W for power, A for current, and V for voltage.

2. The data refreshes about every 5 minutes. It is recommended that the endpoint call frequency is no less than 5 minutes.

p+point_id.description: Measuring Point Value (p1 represents the value of point_id=1)

dev_fault_status.description: Device fault alarm status 1: Fault 2: Alarm 4: normal

illegal_ps_key_list.description: Invalid ps_key Collection (returned when ps_key_list is passed)

illgal_sn_list.description: Invalid sn Collection (returned when sn_list is passed)

ps_key_list.description: ps_key Collection (must pass the same device type)

sn_list.description: SN Collection (pass either the ps_key_list or sn_list)

point_id_list.description: Measuring Point ID Set (see Appendix 10 for specific open measuring point definitions)

is_get_point_dict.description: Return Measuring Point Dictionary? (0: No; 1: Yes; defaults to no)

post.summary: Query the device parameter setting history
post.description: Query the parameter setting history by the device UUID and parameter code.

returnValue.description: Returned value (indicates parameter readback values during parameter readback)

commandStatus.description: Task Status (1: Pending Execution; 2: Executing; 4: Success; 5: Failure; 6: Timeout; 9: Canceled)

setPrecision.description: Parameter precision (please refer to measuring point description for details)

unit.description: Parameter unit (please refer to measuring point description for details)

taskSource.description: Task source : 1 = Page delivery , 2 = Open API

createTime.description: Task detail creation time , format : yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss

updateTime.description: Task detail update time , format : yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss

paramCode.description: Parameter Code (refer to the control parameter code definition)

paramList.description: Parameter Code collection (refer to the control parameter code definition)  , Maximum Quantity: 100

startTime.description: Start Time , format : yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss . Start and end time span must not exceed 30 days

endTime.description: End Time , format : yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss  . Start and end time span must not exceed 30 days

post.description: Query device fault alarm information.

fault_type.description: Fault Type: (1: Fault; 2: Alarm; 3: Notice; 4: Suggestion; separate multiple values with commas)

fault_reason.description: The device's actual fault code (separate multiple values with "|")

process_status.description: Fault Handling Status: (1: Unconfirmed; 2: Pending; 3: Processing; 4: Resolved; 9: Closed)

ps_id.description: Plant ID (if the ps_id is not passed, the fault information of all authorized plants is queried by default)

fault_type.description: Fault Type: (1: Fault; 2: Alarm; 3: Notice; 4: Suggestion; separate multiple values with commas)

fault_code.description: Unique Fault Identifier (pass to query the fault alarm information)

fault_name.description: Fault Name (fuzzy search if not empty)

process_status.description: Fault Status: (8: Unclosed; 9: Closed; 999: Unclosed and Closed (cannot query across months); queries for unclosed faults if empty)

startTime.description: Start Time (format: yyyyMMddHHmm) can be empty. Note: When querying the faults with process_status=9 or 999, the time range must be limited to a single month and cannot span across months. If no time is provided, the query will default to faults for the current month.

endTime.description: End Time (format: yyyyMMddHHmm) can be empty. Note: When querying the faults with process_status=9 or 999, the time range must be limited to a single month and cannot span across months. If no time is provided, the query will default to faults for the current month.

post.summary: Query the historical datapoint datapoint of the optimizer
post.description: Query the historical measurement point datas of the optimizer according to the start time and end time, device ps_key, and data type to obtain the device datas related data, support multi-measurement point query. Note: The unit of the data returned by the interface is the smallest unit, such as the unit corresponding to the power generation data is the smallest unit wh, and the unit corresponding to the power data is the smallest unit w.

{data_type}.description: The query data_type (such as: 1). Refers to the data_type input parameter

ps_key_list.description: Optimizer ps_key, supports up to 100 at a time

data_point.description: The format is p+measuring point, separating multiple measuring points with commas. Example: If the ps_key of the queried device is an inverter, then p1 represents the inverter yield (in Wh), p2 represents the inverter cumulative yield (in Wh), and p24 represents the inverter total active power (in W). See  Common Measuring Points for specific open measuring point definitions.

start_time.description: Note for Start Time: When query_type is "day," the date format is yyyyMMdd. When query_type is "month," the date format is yyyyMM. When query_type is "year," the date format is yyyy.

end_time.description: Note for End Time: When query_type is "day," the date format is yyyyMMdd. When query_type is "month," the date format is yyyyMM. When query_type is "year," the date format is yyyy.

data_type.description: 1: Mean; 2: Peak; 3: Trough; 4: Total (only monthly and yearly data has a "total" value). Separate multiple values with commas. If data can be aggregated, then: data_type is 2 when querying daily data, and 4 when querying monthly or yearly data

query_type.description: Query type: Daily data: 1 Monthly data: 2 Annual data: 3

post.summary: Gets a list of optimizer devices under the power plant
post.description: Gets a list of optimizer devices under the power plant

code.description: 1: normal; 9: No permission

post.summary: Queries the minute level data of the historical stations of the optimizer
post.description: The query optimizer retrips the device minute data for use by third-party platforms. Note: The time span cannot exceed 30 days. Note: The unit of the data returned by the interface is the smallest unit, such as the unit corresponding to the power generation data is the smallest unit wh, and the unit corresponding to the power data is the smallest unit w.

{p+point_id}.description: Measuring Point Value (output parameters are as follows: p1, p83022)

time_stamp.description: Time (time zone where the plant is located)

points.description: Test point id. Multiple test points are separated by commas (,). Currently only supports p58101, p58103 p58104, p58105, p58106, p58107

ps_key_list.description: Optimizer ps_key, supports up to 100 at a time

minute_interval.description: Duration Interval in Minutes (15 means measuring point data is queried every 15 minutes; if the parameter is not passed or empty, measuring point data is queried every 5 minutes)

post.description: Query optimizer's real-time data API

p+point_id.description: Measuring Point Value (p1 represents the value of point_id=1)

ps_key_list.description: List of PS_keys of the optimizer, not more than 100 in a single query

point_id_list.description: Need to query the measuring point list, now support point list: 58101581 03581 04581 05581 06581

post.description: Query the parameter configuration result via the parameter configuration task ID.

create_time_zone.description: Task Creation Time in User Time Zone (format: yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ssXXX)

over_time_zone.description: Task Completion Time in User Time Zone (format: yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ssXXX)

command_status.description: Parameter Instruction Status: (1: Pending Execution; 2: Executing; 4: Success; 5: Failure; 6: Timeout)

create_time_zone.description: Creation time in the user’s time zone. Format: yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ssXXX

param_code.description: Parameter Code (refer to the control parameter code definition)

return_value.description: Returned value (indicates parameter readback values during parameter readback)

set_precision.description: Parameter precision (please refer to measuring point description for details)

set_val_name.description: Name of values in the parameter range

unit.description: Parameter unit (please refer to measuring point description for details)

update_time_zone.description: Update Time in User Time Zone (format: yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ssXXX)

post.description: Query basic plant info by plant ID. Supports batch queries.

code.description: Endpoint Response Code: (0: Query failed, endpoint exception; 1: Success; 4: The ps_ids was empty and no plants were found via sns; 5: The queried plants or sns exceeds the limit; 6: Could not find any basic plant info via ps_ids; 7: No authorized plants in ps_ids

not_authorization_ps_list.description: Unauthorized Plant ID Collection in ps_ids

ps_type.description: Plant Type: 1: Utility Plant; 3: Distributed PV Plant; 4: Residential PV Plant; 5: Residential Energy Storage Plant; 6: Village Plant; 7: Distributed Energy Storage Plant; 8: Poverty Alleviation Plant; 9: Wind Power Plant; 12: C&I Energy Storage Plant

build_status.description: Plant Construction Status (0: Not Started; 1: Under Construction; 2: Connected; 3: Planned; 4: Not Connected)

connect_type.description: Grid Connection Type: (1: Full Grid; 2: Self-Generated and Self-Used (Surplus Power); 3: Self-Generated and Self-Used (No Feed Network); 4: Off-Grid)

ps_fault_status.description: Plant Fault Status (1: Fault; 2: Alarm; 3: Normal)

ps_ids.description: The plant ID, separate multiple values with commas (can query up to 20 IDs—either the ps_ids or sns must be provided; when both are passed, the ps_ids is queried; the sns is only used if the ps_ids query returns empty)

sns.description: Communication Device SN (separate multiple values with commas (you can query up to 20 SNs); the ps_ids is queried and the sns is only used if the ps_ids query returns empty)

is_get_ps_remarks.description: Get Plant Remarks?: (1: Yes; defaults to no if not passed)

post.summary: Query Historical Daily/Monthly/Annual Measuring Point Data of Plants
post.description:
Query the daily/monthly/yearly data of plant measuring points based on the start time, end time, ps_id, and data type of plants. Supports queries of multiple measuring points and up to 50 plants.

Note: This endpoint always returns the smallest unit of data. For example, Wh for yield and W for power. Supports up to 100 days for daily data queries, 24 months for monthly data queries, and 5 years for yearly data queries. Historical data endpoints do not return data for the current day.

{data_type}.description: The query data_type (such as: 1). Refers to the data_type input parameter

query_type.description: Query Type: (Query Daily Data: day; Query Monthly Data: month; Query Yearly Data: year)

data_type.description: 1: Mean; 2: Peak; 3: Trough; 4: Total (only monthly and yearly data has a "total" value). Separate multiple values with commas. If data can be aggregated, then: data_type is 2 when querying daily data, and 4 when querying monthly or yearly data

data_point.description: The format is p+measuring point, separating multiple measuring points with commas. Example: If the ps_key of the queried device is an inverter, then p1 represents the inverter yield (in Wh), p2 represents the inverter cumulative yield (in Wh), and p24 represents the inverter total active power (in W). See Appendix 10 for specific open measuring point definitions.

start_time.description: Note for Start Time: When query_type is "day," the date format is yyyyMMdd. When query_type is "month," the date format is yyyyMM. When query_type is "year," the date format is yyyy.

end_time.description: Note for End Time: When query_type is "day," the date format is yyyyMMdd. When query_type is "month," the date format is yyyyMM. When query_type is "year," the date format is yyyy.

is_get_point_dict.description: Return Measuring Point Dictionary? (0: No; 1: Yes; defaults to no)

post.summary: Query Historical Minute-Level Measuring Point Data of Plants
post.description: Query the minute-level data of plant measuring points based on the plant ps_id, measuring point ID, and the specified time interval (time zone where the plant is located). Supports time intervals of up to 3 hours and up to 50 plants. The endpoint will not return measuring points with no data. Supports up to 50 measuring points per request. Historical data endpoints do not return data for the current day.

time_stamp.description: Time (time zone where the plant is located)

start_time_stamp.description: Start Time (time zone where the plant is located; format: yyyyMMddHHmmss)

end_time_stamp.description: End Time (time zone where the plant is located; format: yyyyMMddHHmmss)

minute_interval.description: Duration Interval in Minutes (15 means measuring point data is queried every 15 minutes; if the parameter is not passed or empty, measuring point data is queried every 5 minutes)

points.description: p+ Measuring Point ID (such as p3022 or p3024, separate multiple measuring points with commas; see Appendix 10 for specific open measuring point definitions)

is_get_point_dict.description: Return Measuring Point Dictionary? (0: No; 1: Yes; defaults to no)

post.summary: Query plant real-time measuring point data
post.description:
Query plant real-time measuring point data by plant ps_id.

Note:
1. This endpoint always returns the smallest unit of data. For example, Wh for yield, W for power, A for current, and V for voltage.

2. The data refreshes about every 5 minutes. It is recommended that the endpoint call frequency is no less than 5 minutes.

p+point_id.description: Measuring Point Value (p1 represents the value of point_id=1)

point_id_list.description: Measuring Point ID Set (see Appendix 10 for specific open measuring point definitions)

is_get_point_dict.description: Return Measuring Point Dictionary? (0: No; 1: Yes; defaults to no)

post.description: Read the task ID through the read-only parameter to query the task execution result.

uuid.description: The device uuid (separate multiple values with commas). When set_type=2, only single-device operations are supported.

code.description: Operation Result

command_status.description: Task Status (1: Pending Execution; 2: Executing; 4: Success; 5: Failure; 6: Timeout; 9: Canceled)

post.description: Sends the device's parameter configuration information.

check_result.description: Error Code (overall verification): (0: Verification failed and configuration could not be performed; 1: Task dispatch successful; 2: Duplicate parameter configuration measuring point; 3: Parameter limit exceeded; 4: Parameter configuration measuring point ID or value empty; 5: Device does not exist; 6: Parameter configuration template not set; 7: Device offline; 8: Timeout period exceeds range of [0-1800] seconds; 9: Operation repeated; 11: Only single-device operations are supported for parameter readback; 12: There is no support for negative values on this device's current version. Please upgrade to Logger1000 or reconfigure the settings.)

code.description: Error Code (single-device): (0: Verification failed and configuration could not be performed; 1: Task dispatch successful; 2: Duplicate parameter configuration measuring point; 3: Parameter limit exceeded; 4: Parameter configuration measuring point ID or value empty; 5: Device does not exist; 6: Parameter configuration template not set; 6-1: The device model doesn't support current param-setting code or user has no authority to this param-setting code; 7: Device offline)

uuid.description: The device uuid (separate multiple values with commas). When set_type=2, only single-device operations are supported.

not_support_negative_num_uuids.description: When check_result is 12, it returns the UUID of the device that does not support copy settings.

set_type.description: Parameter Configuration Type: (0: Configuration; 2: Readback; defaults to 0)

uuid.description: The device uuid (separate multiple values with commas). When set_type=2, only single-device operations are supported.

expire_second.description: Timeout Period of Parameter Configuration Task (in seconds, 0 to 1800)

param_list.description: Configured Parameter: The data type of each element is Map, the keys are param_code and set_value, and the values are the parameter's measuring point ID and configured value

param_code.description: Parameter Code (refer to the control parameter code definition)

set_value.description: Parameter Configured Value (empty during readback; else must be passed)

post.description: Verifies whether the current device supports parameter configuration.

check_result.description: Verification Status (refers to overall parameter verification; 0: Failed; 1: Passed; 5: Device Does Not Exist; 6: Device Offline)
Verification Status (refers to single-device parameter verification; 0: Failed; 1: Passed; 5: Device Does Not Exist; 6: Device Offline)

uuid.description: The device uuid (separate multiple values with commas). When set_type=2, only single-device operations are supported.

post.description: Query Authorized Plants

ps_type.description: Plant Type: 1: Utility Plant; 3: Distributed PV Plant; 4: Residential PV Plant; 5: Residential Energy Storage Plant; 6: Village Plant; 7: Distributed Energy Storage Plant; 8: Poverty Alleviation Plant; 9: Wind Power Plant; 12: C&I Energy Storage Plant

ps_fault_status.description: Plant Fault Status (1: Fault; 2: Alarm; 3: Normal)

connect_type.description: Grid Connection Type: (1: Full Grid; 2: Self-Generated and Self-Used (Surplus Power); 3: Self-Generated and Self-Used (No Feed Network); 4: Off-Grid)

build_status.description: Plant Construction Status (0: Not Started; 1: Under Construction; 2: Connected; 3: Planned; 4: Not Connected)

ps_type.description: Plant Type (separate multiple values with commas, all values are queried by default): 1: Utility Plant; 3: Distributed PV Plant; 4: Residential PV Plant; 5: Residential Energy Storage Plant; 6: Village Plant; 7: Distributed Energy Storage Plant; 8: Poverty Alleviation Plant; 9: Wind Power Plant; 12: C&I Energy Storage Plant

valid_flag.description: Plant Status: (1: Normal; 2: Disabled; 3: Connected). If not passed, plants with a status of 1 are queried. Separate values with commas to query multiple statuses

post.description: Dispatches a read-only parameter read task.

code.description: Error Code (single-device): (1: Successful; 2: Device Offline or Does Not Exist; 3: Already Dispatched; 4: Device Cannot Get This Read-Only Measuring Point; 5: Communication Device Does Not Support Read-Only Parameters)

uuid.description: The device uuid (separate multiple values with commas). When set_type=2, only single-device operations are supported.

post.description: Dispatch Read-Only Parameter Read Task

code.description: Error Code (single-device): (1: Successful; 2: Device Offline or Does Not Exist; 3: Already Dispatched; 4: Device Cannot Get This Read-Only Measuring Point; 5: Communication Device Does Not Support Read-Only Parameters)

uuid.description: The device uuid (separate multiple values with commas). When set_type=2, only single-device operations are supported.

post.description: The owner unshares the power plant he shares, or accepts the unsharing initiated by the user who shares the power plant.

code.description: Result code: 1: Successfully unshare; 2: The user account shared by the power plant does not exist; 3: The power plant is not under the user who accepts the power plant sharing, and the sharing cannot be lifted;

release_type.description: Type: 1: The owner unshares the accept_user_account. 2: Unshare initiated by the user who accepts the share of the power plant; Default 2

accept_user_account.description: The user account shared by the power plant; This parameter is mandatory when release_type is equal to 1

post.description: Add a user as a channel partner of a power plant to support batch operations.

result_code.description: Operation result Code: 0: The failure is caused by unknown reasons. Contact the administrator. 1: Success; 2: The ps_id_list collection is incorrectly entered. The passed ps_id_list contains duplicate power plants, or the ps_id does not exist, or the ps_id does not belong to the operator. 4: Only one item of added person information can be passed; 5: Channels/partners already exist; 6: The user has the permission to browse or manage the current power plant. 7: The number of power plants (management permission) in the operated power plant has exceeded the upper limit. 8: The user has the permission to browse or manage the current power plant. 9: Channels/partners already exist; 11: The added person does not exist

user_email.description: The email account, email address, and mobile phone number of the added person can be sent only one item at most

share_type.description: Permission types of added channel partners: 1: browse permission 2: manage permission

is_channel_partner.description: Add as channel partner, fixed transfer 1

post.summary: Add power plant channel partners through inverter sn and nmi
post.description: Add channel partners in batches through inverter sn and nmi, and send up to 50 sets of data. After successful addition, a notification email will be sent to the power plant owner and installer (only applicable to Australia).

code.description: Error type 0- The sn parameter is passed in, and the nmi is empty. 1- The sn or nmi is repeated. 2- The nmi is different from the power plant queried by the sn. 3- The power plant already exists under the user. 4- The length of the nmi cannot be less than 10

list.description: sn and nmi sets, up to 50 groups are passed in

post.description: The share of the power plant is carried out through the authorized account of the power plant, the SN of a device under the power plant, and the user account receiving the share.

code.description: Operation result code: 1: The sharing succeeds 2: The corresponding power plant cannot be queried according to the device sn 3: the user who initiated the sharing does not exist 4: the user who received the sharing does not exist 5: The power plant corresponding to the device sn does not belong to the user who has received the sharing and cannot be shared 6: The power plant corresponding to the device sn has been shared by the user. Type of power plant sharing that is not supported

accept_user_account.description: The account of the user who received the share from the power plant (accept the share)

owner_user_account.description: Account of the user who initiated the sharing of the power plant (power plant owner)

share_type.description: Permission type of added channel partner: 1: browse permission 2: Manage permission Default value: 2
